notes: fiscal_kpis was empty for CBRL, so there are no fiscal.ai-kpi candidates; all 67 candidates come from 8-K/10-K/10-Q/transcript text.
Event CBRL_2024-12-04T11.00 (transcript dated 2024-12-04) contains another company's earnings call (111 Inc.), not Cracker Barrel; it was excluded from mining and counted as skipped.

19 events carry only form/item labels with no body text (Reg FD 7.01 disclosures incl. the +8.2% 2026-01-12 event, officer 5.02 changes, shareholder-vote 5.07 results, financing 1.01/2.03 8-Ks, and an Item 8.01 8-K) and were skipped as vague; the financing 8-Ks of 2025-06-16 and 2025-05-16 are instead covered via the convertible_notes_issuance evidence quoted from the 2025-09-17 press release.

Skipped vague or rule-violating candidate ideas: capital_allocation (too broad), guest_frequency and retail_assortment (thin evidence), free_cash_flow (source says only 'strong cash flow'), per-commodity names like poultry/egg prices (covered by commodity_inflation), age-cohort traffic (any faithful name needs numbers), discounting level (covered by menu_pricing/menu_promotions), Dolly Parton partnership (person name banned), shareholder_vote (item label only).
store_closures and store_impairment intentionally share one evidence sentence that bundles both causal variables ('impairment charges and store closure costs'); split into two drivers per R2.
All 67 evidence quotes were programmatically verified as exact substrings of the named event's content field.
